comment_text.sn8.10:5.1: Emend anekākārasampannaṁ to anekākārasampannā per Bodhi’s Connected Discourses, note 527.
comment_text.sn8.10:5.4: I take aṅga (“factor”) as implying “path factor”, and ākāra (“attribute”) as indicating the Buddha’s qualities.

root_text.sn8.10:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati isigilipasse kāḷasilāyaṁ mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ pañcamattehi bhikkhusatehi sabbeheva arahantehi.
root_text.sn8.10:1.2: Tesaṁ sudaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno cetasā cittaṁ samannesati vippamuttaṁ nirupadhiṁ.
root_text.sn8.10:1.4: “ayaṁ kho bhagavā rājagahe viharati isigilipasse kāḷasilāyaṁ mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ pañcamattehi bhikkhusatehi sabbeheva arahantehi.
root_text.sn8.10:1.5: Tesaṁ sudaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno cetasā cittaṁ samannesati vippamuttaṁ nirupadhiṁ.
root_text.sn8.10:1.6: Yannūnāhaṁ āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ bhagavato sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthaveyyan”ti.
root_text.sn8.10:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn8.10:2.4: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ bhagavato sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthavi:

translation_text.sn8.10:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying on the slopes of Isigili at the Black Rock, together with a large Saṅgha of five hundred mendicants, all of whom were perfected ones.
translation_text.sn8.10:1.2: Thereupon, with his mind, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna checked to see whose mind was liberated and free of attachments.
translation_text.sn8.10:1.4: “The Buddha is staying on the slopes of Isigili … with five hundred perfected ones.
translation_text.sn8.10:1.5: Mahāmoggallāna is checking to see whose mind is liberated and free of attachments.
translation_text.sn8.10:1.6: Why don’t I extoll him in the Buddha’s presence with fitting verses?”
translation_text.sn8.10:2.1: Then Venerable Vaṅgīsa got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.sn8.10:2.2: “I feel inspired to speak, Blessed One! I feel inspired to speak, Holy One!”
translation_text.sn8.10:2.3: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said the Buddha.
translation_text.sn8.10:2.4: Then Vaṅgīsa extolled Mahāmoggallāna in his presence with fitting verses:
translation_text.sn8.10:3.1: “As the sage, who has gone beyond suffering,
translation_text.sn8.10:3.3: he is revered by disciples with the three knowledges,
translation_text.sn8.10:5.1: That is how those endowed <j>with many fine qualities

variant_text.sn8.10:4.1: Te cetasā → cetasā (19Th1:1626) | anupariyeti → anupariyesati (sya-all, km)
